Official_Indication: MDD in adults who failed ≥1 antidepressant; OCD adjunct in adults; anxious depression
Intended_Use_Text: The NeuroStar Advanced Therapy System is indicated for the treatment of depressive episodes and for decreasing anxiety symptoms for those who may exhibit comorbid anxiety symptoms in adult patients suffering from Major Depressive Disorder who have failed to achieve satisfactory improvement from prior antidepressant medication in the current episode.
Key_Tech_Specs: 10 Hz rTMS; 120% RMT; 3000 pulses/session; 37.5 min treatment
Contraindications: Metallic implants near coil; cochlear implants; cardiac pacemakers; seizure history (relative)
Adverse_Event_Notes: Headache; scalp discomfort; seizure risk ~1/10,000 sessions
Notes: OCD clearance K212289 (2022). Anxious depression added with K230029.

Official_Indication: MDD in adults; OCD adjunct in adults; smoking cessation; MDD adjunct adolescents 15-21
Intended_Use_Text: Transcranial magnetic stimulation system for obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD De Novo). MDD: treatment of depressive episodes and decreasing comorbid anxiety symptoms in adults; adjunct for adolescents 15-21.
Key_Tech_Specs: H7-coil targets ACC/mPFC for OCD; H4-coil targets bilateral insula/PFC for smoking; H1-coil for depression
Contraindications: Metallic implants near coil; implanted electronic devices; seizure disorder
Adverse_Event_Notes: Headache; scalp discomfort; jaw pain; transient anxiety during OCD provocation phase
Notes: First De Novo for OCD (Aug 2018). Smoking cessation cleared 2020. Adolescent MDD Nov 2025.

Official_Indication: MDD in adults; OCD adjunct in adults; MDD adjunct adolescents 15-21
Intended_Use_Text: MagVenture TMS Therapy system is indicated for treatment of major depressive disorder in adult patients who have failed to receive satisfactory improvement from prior antidepressant medication. Adjunct treatment of OCD in adults. Adjunct MDD therapy for adolescents 15-21.
Key_Tech_Specs: Supports 10 Hz rTMS and iTBS protocols; multiple coil options
Contraindications: Metallic implants near coil; implanted electronic devices; seizure disorder
Adverse_Event_Notes: Headache; scalp discomfort; mild facial twitching
Notes: iTBS-capable. OCD clearance 2020. Adolescent MDD Aug 2025.

Official_Indication: MDD in adults; OCD adjunct in adults
Intended_Use_Text: The Horizon 3.0 TMS Therapy System is a non-invasive tool for the stimulation of cortical neurons for the treatment of Major Depressive Disorder and OCD adjunct.
Key_Tech_Specs: rTMS and iTBS capable; optional neuronavigation
Contraindications: Metallic implants near coil; implanted electronic devices; seizure disorder

Official_Indication: MDD in adults; OCD adjunct in adults (2025)
Intended_Use_Text: The Apollo TMS Therapy System is indicated for the treatment of Major Depressive Disorder in adult patients who have failed to achieve satisfactory improvement from prior antidepressant medication in the current episode.
Contraindications: Metallic implants near coil; implanted electronic devices; seizure disorder
Notes: OCD adjunct clearance announced Aug 2025; specific 510(k) number to verify.

Regulatory_Status: FDA cleared (510(k)) — to verify specific numbers
Regulatory_Pathway: 510(k) — exact numbers not confirmed in FDA database
Official_Indication: OCD in adults; MDD using iTBS protocol
Intended_Use_Text: Treatment of adult patients diagnosed with obsessive-compulsive disorder. Treatment of depression using intermittent theta-burst stimulation.
Key_Tech_Specs: iTBS and standard rTMS protocols; cloud connectivity
Contraindications: Metallic implants near coil; implanted electronic devices; seizure disorder
Notes: 510(k) numbers not confirmed in FDA database. Manufacturer claims clearances. Verify against AccessGFDA.

Official_Indication: Moderate-to-severe MDD in adults ≥18, not treatment-refractory
Intended_Use_Text: FL-100 is intended for the treatment of moderate to severe major depressive disorder in the current episode, either as monotherapy or as an adjunctive treatment, in patients 18 years and older who are not considered treatment refractory to medication.
Key_Tech_Specs: 2.0 mA tDCS; Bluetooth-enabled; smartphone companion app; home-use headset
Contraindications: Metallic implants in current path; implanted electronic devices; skull defects; skin lesions at electrode site
Adverse_Event_Notes: Skin redness; tingling; itching at electrode site; headache (mild); phosphenes (rare)
Notes: ONLY FDA-approved tDCS device (PMA Dec 2025). Product code JXK. CE-marked in EU prior.

Intended_Use_Text: Cranial Electrotherapy Stimulator to Treat Insomnia And/Or Anxiety (K903014). Depression is NOT an FDA-cleared indication in the US.
Electrode_or_Coil_Type: Ear-clip CES electrodes; AS-Trode probes for pain
Key_Tech_Specs: 0.5 Hz modified waveform; subsensory to 600 µA; battery-powered portable
Notes: Depression NOT FDA-cleared in US despite marketing references. CES product code QJQ. Depression indication available outside US.

Intended_Use_Text: Cranial Electrotherapy Stimulator To Treat Insomnia And/Or Anxiety (K903654). Company markets for depression but original 510(k) specifies insomnia/anxiety.
Notes: Class 2 recall Z-1600-2023 for battery overheating risk (FW-100, FW-200). Depression marketing claim exceeds 510(k) cleared indication.

Official_Indication: Epilepsy (partial-onset seizures, ≥4 yrs); Treatment-resistant depression (adults ≥18, ≥4 Rx failures)
Intended_Use_Text: Epilepsy: adjunctive therapy reducing frequency of seizures in patients 4 years and older with partial onset seizures refractory to antiepileptic medications. Depression: adjunctive long-term treatment of chronic or recurrent depression for patients 18 years or older experiencing a major depressive episode with inadequate response to four or more adequate antidepressant treatments.
Channels: Single lead on left vagus nerve
Electrode_or_Coil_Type: Helical bipolar electrode on left cervical vagus nerve
Key_Tech_Specs: Implantable pulse generator (IPG); programmable; AutoStim cardiac-based seizure detection; MRI-conditional (some models)
Contraindications: Prior left cervical vagotomy; caution with sleep apnea and swallowing disorders
Adverse_Event_Notes: Hoarseness (~70%); cough; dyspnea; throat discomfort; surgical risks (infection, nerve injury)
Notes: PMA approved: epilepsy Jul 1997; depression Jul 2005 (S050). Age expanded to ≥4 yrs (S198, Apr 2017). >125,000 implanted worldwide.

Official_Indication: Cluster headache (acute + prevention); Migraine (acute + prevention, ≥12 yrs); Paroxysmal hemicrania; Hemicrania continua
Intended_Use_Text: Adjunctive treatment to prevent and acutely treat cluster headache in adults. Acute and preventive treatment of migraine in adolescents ≥12 and adults. Treatment of paroxysmal hemicrania and hemicrania continua in adults.
Electrode_or_Coil_Type: Gel-coated contact surface applied to neck
Key_Tech_Specs: Handheld device; 2×120-second stimulations per attack; rechargeable; prescribed dose limits
Contraindications: Active implantable medical device (pacemaker, ICD); carotid atherosclerosis/surgery; vagus nerve abnormality
Adverse_Event_Notes: Neck twitching; voice changes; cough; tickling; bradycardia (rare)
Notes: Cervical nVNS, NOT auricular. Initial clearance Apr 2017 (episodic CH). Migraine Jan 2018. CE-marked broader.

Regulatory_Pathway: PMA P960009; HDE H020007 (dystonia); HDE H050003 (OCD)
Official_Indication: Essential tremor; Parkinson's disease; Dystonia; OCD (HDE); Epilepsy (ANT-DBS)
Intended_Use_Text: ET: unilateral thalamic stimulation for suppression of tremor. PD: bilateral STN/GPi stimulation as adjunctive therapy. Dystonia: bilateral GPi/STN stimulation for drug-refractory primary dystonia. OCD (HDE): bilateral ALIC stimulation as adjunct to medications. Epilepsy: bilateral ANT stimulation for partial-onset seizures refractory to ≥3 AEDs.
Key_Tech_Specs: Percept PC with BrainSense technology; adaptive DBS (aDBS) approved Feb 2025 (ADAPT-PD); MRI-conditional
Contraindications: Significant dementia; active major psychiatric illness (relative); diathermy contraindicated
Adverse_Event_Notes: Surgical: hemorrhage (1-3%), infection; stimulation: dysarthria, gait instability, mood changes; hardware complications
Population: Adults (all); Children ≥7 (dystonia); Children ≥12 (primary generalized dystonia PMA)
Notes: ET approved Jul 1997. PD Jan 2002. Dystonia HDE 2003 → PMA 2025. OCD HDE Feb 2009. Epilepsy May 2018. Adaptive DBS Feb 2025.

Intended_Use_Text: Bilateral STN/GPi stimulation as adjunctive therapy to reduce symptoms of advanced levodopa-responsive PD. Unilateral or bilateral VIM stimulation for suppression of disabling upper extremity tremor in adult ET patients.
Key_Tech_Specs: First directional DBS system; multiple independent current control (MICC); MRI-conditional
Contraindications: Significant dementia; active major psychiatric illness (relative); diathermy contraindicated
Adverse_Event_Notes: Surgical: hemorrhage, infection; stimulation: paresthesias, dysarthria, gait issues
Notes: Original approval Sep 2016. GPi for PD expanded Jan 2020 (S039). CE-marked ~30 countries.

Device_Name: Boston Scientific Vercise Genus DBS System
Official_Indication: Parkinson's disease (STN, GPi) — US only for PD
Intended_Use_Text: Bilateral STN stimulation in the treatment of patients with moderate to advanced levodopa-responsive Parkinson's disease. GPi added with Vercise Genus (S034, Jan 2021).
Key_Tech_Specs: Vercise Genus: directional; 1.5T full-body MRI conditional; rechargeable and non-rechargeable options
Contraindications: Significant dementia; active major psychiatric illness (relative); diathermy contraindicated
Adverse_Event_Notes: Surgical: hemorrhage, infection; stimulation: dyskinesia, dysarthria; hardware complications
Notes: US: PD only. EU label broader (includes dystonia, ET). Original approval Dec 2017. Genus Jan 2021.

Official_Indication: Relaxation training using alpha EEG biofeedback
Intended_Use_Text: Relaxation training using alpha EEG biofeedback. NOT cleared for ADHD, seizures, anxiety disorders, depression, or other clinical conditions.
Key_Tech_Specs: 2-channel EEG acquisition; software-based feedback protocols; multiple training modalities available
Contraindications: No specific device contraindications beyond standard EEG; active epilepsy (relative for training protocols)
Notes: Most neurofeedback devices are 510(k)-exempt under 21 CFR 882.5050. Marketing claims may exceed cleared indication.

Intended_Use_Text: The Sparrow Ascent is a transcutaneous nerve field stimulator intended to be used in patients experiencing opioid withdrawal in conjunction with standard symptomatic medications and other therapies for opioid withdrawal symptoms under the supervision of trained clinical personnel.
Contraindications: Active implanted vagal nerve stimulators; cardiac arrhythmias (caution)
Adverse_Event_Notes: Ear discomfort; skin irritation; cough reflex
Notes: Product code PZR (percutaneous nerve stimulator for SUD). NO psychiatric indication clearance. Only FDA-cleared taVNS device.

Regulatory_Pathway: CE marking (Dec 2018); No FDA pathway in US
Official_Indication: EU: Treatment of neurological diseases including Alzheimer's disease. US: Investigational/research only.
Intended_Use_Text: EU CE-marked for treatment of neurological diseases via transcranial pulse stimulation. NOT FDA cleared or approved for any indication in the US.
Key_Tech_Specs: Focused ultrasound single pulses; neuronavigation; claimed deeper penetration than TMS
Contraindications: To verify — limited published safety data for long-term use
Adverse_Event_Notes: Headache (mild); sensation during treatment; thermal effects (theoretical at higher intensities)
Notes: CE-marked EU only. US research/investigational. Some US clinics charged patients for unapproved use per STAT News 2022.

Regulatory_Status: NOT FDA cleared or approved; FDA-registered facility only
Regulatory_Pathway: General wellness device classification (manufacturer claim); No 510(k) or PMA
Official_Indication: No FDA-cleared indication. Marketed as general wellness device.
Intended_Use_Text: No FDA clearance or approval for neurological or psychiatric indications. Company states: 'Vielight devices do not have FDA approval.' Marketed under general wellness guidance.
Key_Tech_Specs: 810nm wavelength; pulsed at 40 Hz (gamma); intranasal + transcranial modules
Contraindications: To verify — limited regulatory safety review
Adverse_Event_Notes: Headache; warmth at application site; eye safety with NIR
Notes: NOT FDA cleared. Health Canada license for respiratory symptoms only. Clinical trials ongoing for AD.

Official_Indication: Acute and preventive treatment of migraine in adults and adolescents ≥12
Intended_Use_Text: Single-pulse TMS for the acute and preventive treatment of migraine headache in patients 12 years and older.
Electrode_or_Coil_Type: Toroidal coil (placed at back of head)
Key_Tech_Specs: Single magnetic pulse; portable; battery-powered; patient-activated; brief pulse (<1 ms)
Contraindications: Metallic implants in or near head; cardiac pacemakers; cochlear implants; seizure medications at high doses
Adverse_Event_Notes: Lightheadedness (mild); very low seizure risk (single pulse)
Notes: FDA cleared 2013/2014 for acute migraine. Expanded for preventive use. Prescription required.
